title: Manage Gradle builds deliberately
description: Pin SDK/dependency versions, use build variants for environments, and keep secrets out of the build instead of guessing config.
body: Use when configuring the Android build. Do not use dynamic '+' dependency versions, and do not commit signing keys or API secrets into Gradle/source. Actions: pin compileSdk/targetSdk/minSdk intentionally, lock dependency versions (use the version catalog/BOM), separate environments with build variants/flavors, and inject secrets via Gradle properties or the environment, not committed files. Validation signals include reproducible builds across machines and CI, and no secret literals in the repo. Risk boundary: dynamic versions make builds non-reproducible and can pull a breaking transitive update, while a committed signing key or secret is a security incident and a release-integrity risk — treat build config and secrets as release-critical.
